title: CAN SLIM: seven letters, one checklist

diagnosticQuestions.prompt: CAN SLIM is best described as:
choices: A checklist for growth-style stock selection (O’Neil framework) — not a guarantee
A type of option spread only
diagnosticQuestions.hint: It ties current and annual earnings, newness, supply/demand, leadership, sponsorship, and market direction.

diagnosticQuestions.prompt: The “M” in CAN SLIM usually emphasizes:
choices: Market direction — what the major indexes are doing
diagnosticQuestions.hint: Even strong stocks can struggle in a bad tape.

teachSlides.title: C & A — earnings engine
teachSlides.body: C looks at current quarterly earnings growth. A looks at annual growth over several years. Strong growth is a starting filter — not the only input.

teachSlides.title: N — something new
teachSlides.body: New products, new management, or new highs can matter because change can drive attention and re-rating — but hype can also mislead.

teachSlides.title: S & L — supply, demand, leadership
teachSlides.body: Volume at key pivots shows demand. Relative strength vs the market helps separate leaders from laggards — strength matters in momentum regimes.

teachSlides.title: I & M — sponsorship and tape
teachSlides.body: Institutional quality matters to many investors. And if the market is in a correction, even good names can fail — M is your weather report.

recapSlide.title: Recap
recapSlide.body: CAN SLIM bundles earnings, novelty, volume, leadership, sponsorship, and market direction into one repeatable scan. You still need size, stops, and discipline.

masteryQuestions.prompt: A stock shows ~10% current earnings growth and weak relative strength vs the market. A strict CAN SLIM mindset might:
choices: At minimum question whether it meets the “C” and “L” spirit — often weak candidates fail multiple letters
masteryQuestions.explanation: The framework checks several boxes; weak growth and weak leadership often fail the story.

masteryQuestions.prompt: RSI in the 40s for a stock (example) with mediocre earnings growth suggests:
choices: It may not act like a leader — context matters vs the whole checklist
CAN SLIM is only about RSI
masteryQuestions.explanation: Leadership and earnings are broader than one number — but weak RS is a yellow flag in many scans.

choices: Individual stocks and the broad market interact
masteryQuestions.explanation: Tape conditions affect follow-through on setups.
